schema.title: Response List My Memberships Users Me Memberships Get

get.description:
List the active organization's members.

Membership rows are the source of truth (invitation acceptance only
creates a membership), so ``role`` is the member's role in this org —
users have no global role.

patch.description:
Change a member's role within the caller's active organization.

Roles live exclusively on membership rows — this updates the target's
membership in the caller's org. The response's ``role`` mirrors the new
membership role, matching what ``GET /users`` returns.

post.description:
Onboarding entry point for a signed-in user with no organization.

Creates a new `fund_manager_firm` organization and makes the caller its
`admin`, so they can immediately create and manage their own fund without
waiting on an invitation.

get.description:
Return the shared demo organization, or null when none is seeded.

Returns 200 with a null body rather than 404 so the onboarding page can
probe for the demo org without tripping the client's global error
handling.

schema.title: Response Get Demo Organization Organizations Demo Get

post.description:
Onboarding alternative to /self-serve: join the seeded demo org.

Adds the caller as a `fund_manager` of the demo organization so they can
explore the product with realistic pre-seeded data instead of starting
from an empty firm. Idempotent: re-joining returns the existing
membership unchanged (including its original role).

schema.title: Response List All Organizations Superadmin Organizations Get

get.description:
Every user on the platform, across all organizations. `UserRead`
already nests memberships → organization, so the UI can show each
user's orgs and roles without follow-up calls.

schema.title: Response List All Users Superadmin Users Get

post.description:
Re-send the onboarding welcome email to a user on demand. The welcome
template is organization-scoped, so the user must belong to at least one
organization; the first membership's organization is used.

Fire the investor onboarding drip for a user on demand. The drip walks
the reader through the investor portal, so the user must have at least one
LP membership; the first LP membership's organization is used.

schema.title: Response List Organization Members Superadmin Organizations  Organization Id  Members Get
Response List Pending For Me Invitations Pending For Me Get
Response List Fund Groups Fund Groups Get

get.description:
Resolve a fund from its slug within the caller's active organization.

Declared before ``/{fund_id}`` so the literal ``by-slug`` prefix wins the
route match instead of being parsed as a fund UUID.

NAV marks for a fund, newest first. Any member who can view the fund
(LPs included) can read them — the NAV drives their fair-value figures.

schema.title: Response List Fund Valuations Funds  Fund Id  Valuations Get

post.description: Record (or overwrite) the fund NAV for a given as-of date. Managers only.

get.description:
Organizations the caller can enter as an investor (via contact links).
Deliberately membership-free: this is the portal's org switcher source.

schema.title: Response List Investor Organizations Investor Organizations Get
Response List Funds Investor Funds Get
Response List Commitments Investor Commitments Get
Response List Capital Calls Investor Capital Calls Get
Response List Distributions Investor Distributions Get
Response List Documents Investor Documents Get
Response List Communications Investor Communications Get
Response List Investors Investor Investors Get

Response List Investor Contacts Investors  Investor Id  Contacts Get
Response List Commitments For Fund Funds  Fund Id  Commitments Get
Response List Commitments For Investor Investors  Investor Id  Commitments Get
Response List Capital Calls Capital Calls Get

Response List Distributions For Fund Funds  Fund Id  Distributions Get

put.description:
Proxy upload: write the raw request body to the storage backend.

The S3 backend routes uploads here (see ``S3Storage.presign_put``) so the
browser never talks to the bucket directly and no bucket CORS
configuration is needed — the bytes travel through the API, which already
allows the app origins.

post.description:
Queue an AI letter draft from a document.

Runs in the worker (Claude on a multi-page PDF is slow); the draft lands in
the Letters area as a Communication and the requester is notified when
ready. 404 when the feature is off (no ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY``).
Accept raw bytes for a presigned key. Dev-only; protected by a header token.

put.description: Accept raw bytes for a presigned key. Dev-only; protected by a header token.

post.description:
Store an inbound email's attachments as documents for the sender's org.

Always returns 200: a "dropped" result (unknown/ambiguous sender, or nothing
storable) is a normal outcome, not an error. Only a bad/absent shared-secret
token is rejected (403/404).

schema.title: Response List Communications For Fund Funds  Fund Id  Communications Get
Response List Tasks For Fund Funds  Fund Id  Tasks Get

get.description:
List audit events visible to the caller's active membership.

Admins and fund managers see every event in the org; everyone
else only sees events they caused themselves.

schema.title: Response List Audit Logs Audit Logs Get

BankImportListItem.title: BankImportListItem
BankImportListItem.description: Lightweight row for the import-history list (no transactions).

CommunicationRecipientRef.title: CommunicationRecipientRef
CommunicationRecipientRef.description:
Optional override for who should receive a communication.

Either `user_id` or `investor_contact_id` must be set; recipients with both
populated are allowed and treated as a single row keyed on (user, contact).

DocumentDraftLetterResponse.title: DocumentDraftLetterResponse
DocumentDraftLetterResponse.description:
Ack for the async ``POST /documents/{id}/draft-letter`` action.

Drafting runs in the worker; the draft appears in the Letters area once the
job completes and the requester is notified.

InvestorOrganizationRead.title: InvestorOrganizationRead
InvestorOrganizationRead.description:
An organization the user has investor-portal access to.

Access is derived from contact links (``InvestorContact.user_id``), not
membership rows — there may be no membership at all.

InvitationAcceptResponse.title: InvitationAcceptResponse
InvitationAcceptResponse.description:
Result of accepting an invitation.

Deliberately not a membership: lp (investor) invitations grant portal
access purely via contact links and create no membership row at all.

MatchCandidate.title: MatchCandidate
MatchCandidate.description: A suggested capital-call item a bank transaction could settle.

OrgMemberRead.title: OrgMemberRead
OrgMemberRead.description:
A user seen through their membership in the active organization —
``role`` is the per-org membership role (users have no global role).

OrganizationOnboardingCreate.title: OrganizationOnboardingCreate
OrganizationOnboardingCreate.description:
Payload for self-serve onboarding: any signed-in user can found their
own fund manager firm without an invitation. The org `type` is fixed to
`fund_manager_firm` server-side — self-serve signup is only for managers
starting their own fund, not investor firms or service providers, which
still require a superadmin or an invitation.
